title: iberis carnosa subsp. embergeri
intro: Iberis carnosa subsp. embergeri, also known as Corsican candytuft, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its fragrant, white, and pink flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Iberis carnosa subsp. embergeri include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to make teas, salves, and liniments for various health purposes.
